page: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with the stress-ng multi-core test and PassMark CPU score ranking in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory performance is also highly competitive; cached small-block read bandwidth reaches 9076.97 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write speeds rank in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the average range.

From a resource density perspective, the m6a.32xlarge offers high compute and memory capacity on a mature x86_64 architecture, making it a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized workloads that do not require specialized GPU acceleration. A key tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached storage, as the instance lacks local SSDs. This makes it less suitable for applications requiring ultra-low latency local disk I/O, though its 50 Gbps network bandwidth helps mitigate network storage bottlenecks.

This instance configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, large-scale web serving, software compilation, and database operations. Its strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth make it effective for Redis deployments, static web serving, and medium-scale language model prompt processing, while its balanced resource profile supports general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 50 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though single-core metrics remain average. This resource profile offers a balanced, cost-efficient architecture for highly parallelized workloads. It is highly suited for enterprise applications, database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps networking, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose virtual server featuring 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
Equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory at a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth benchmark results
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
